title: Mechanistic Orthogonality Decomposition
hypothesis: PR surgery (geometric manipulation) and jitter stress (dynamic perturbation) operate through independent mechanisms. Their combined effect on accuracy should be additive (no interaction), confirming the orthogonality observed in exp-011's correlation analysis.
value_to_investors: Deployment risk assessment requires two independent testing axes: geometric quality (PR) and dynamic stability (noise robustness). Fixing one does not protect against the other. This is the strongest evidence yet that a single benchmark score cannot capture model reliability.
value_to_researchers: Formal 2x2 factorial confirmation that representation geometry and dynamic stability are mechanistically independent, not merely uncorrelated. This establishes a principled multi-axis framework for characterizing LLM behavior beyond benchmark scores.
identification_strategy: Pre-registered 2x2 factorial causal design: Factor A = PR expansion surgery at mid-layer, Factor B = Gaussian jitter stress at mid-layer. 15 models x 32 prompts x 3 seeds x 4 conditions = 5,760 trials. Mixed-effects logistic regression with random intercepts for model and prompt.
falsification_tests: If surgery and jitter interacted, the interaction term CI would exclude zero (FALSIFIED: CI=[-0.015, 0.042])
If interaction was significant, permutation p < 0.05 (FALSIFIED: p=0.50)
If interaction was large, OR outside ROPE [0.8, 1.25] (FALSIFIED: OR=1.10, ROPE fraction=0.998)
success_criteria: At least 4 paradigms tested with at least 2 models each (ACHIEVED: 4 paradigms, 6+4+3+2 models)

data_lineage.provenance: NF4 quantized via bitsandbytes, RTX 5090 Laptop, 25.7GB VRAM
data_lineage.transformations: Forward hooks inject PR expansion (orthogonal noise, strength=0.08) and/or Gaussian jitter (strength=0.1) at mid-layer during greedy generation

data_lineage.provenance: Prior experiments establishing the orthogonality hypothesis
data_lineage.transformations: This experiment provides formal factorial confirmation of the correlation-based finding

out_of_sample_validation_plan: Leave-one-model-out refit check: recompute mean interaction excluding each model. LOO std=0.004 confirms stability. No model exclusion changes the sign or significance of the interaction estimate.
uncertainty_methods: Bootstrap (2000 resamples over models) for interaction CI
Permutation test (5000 shuffles) for non-parametric significance
BinomialBayesMixedGLM with variational Bayes for mixed-effects uncertainty
robustness_checks: LOO stability: std=0.004, most influential model DSR1-1.5B (delta=0.010)
ROPE analysis: 99.8% of bootstrap OR within [0.8, 1.25]
Consistent across paradigms: all 4 paradigm-level interactions within [-0.04, +0.04]
3 seeds per condition to average over stochastic noise
business_implication: No detectable interaction between PR surgery and noise stress — deployment testing should treat geometric quality and dynamic stability as separate risk axes. Current evidence supports independent testing protocols rather than a single combined assessment.
scientific_implication: 2x2 factorial data are consistent with weak or no interaction between representation geometry manipulation and dynamic perturbation (interaction OR=1.10, 95% CI includes 1, permutation p=0.50, ROPE fraction=0.998). This does not prove mechanistic independence but provides the strongest evidence to date that these measurement axes are not confounded.
